---
title: "DGE"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
# ============================================================
# nf-core/rnaseq gene-level differential expression analysis
# ============================================================

# Install once if needed:
# if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
#   install.packages("BiocManager")
#
# BiocManager::install(c("DESeq2", "pheatmap", "rtracklayer"))
# install.packages(c("ggplot2", "ggrepel", "matrixStats"))
#
# Optional, but recommended for LFC shrinkage:
# BiocManager::install("ashr")

library(DESeq2)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(pheatmap)
library(matrixStats)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 1. Import count matrix
# ----------------------------

count_file <- "salmon.merged.gene_counts.tsv"

dat <- read.delim(
  count_file,
  header = TRUE,
  check.names = FALSE,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

stopifnot(all(c("gene_id", "gene_name") %in% names(dat)))
stopifnot(!anyDuplicated(dat$gene_id))

gene_annotation <- dat[, c("gene_id", "gene_name")]

# Add product descriptions from the EGAPx GTF. Product annotations are
# transcript/isoform-level and are repeated on exon/CDS records, so prefer
# transcript records and collapse distinct isoform products once per gene.
gtf_file <- "complete.genomic.gtf"

if (!requireNamespace("rtracklayer", quietly = TRUE)) {
  stop(
    "Package 'rtracklayer' is required. Install it with: ",
    "BiocManager::install('rtracklayer')"
  )
}

gtf <- rtracklayer::import(gtf_file)

if (!all(c("gene_id", "type", "product") %in% names(mcols(gtf)))) {
  stop("The GTF must contain gene_id, type, and product attributes.")
}

gtf_products <- data.frame(
  gene_id = as.character(mcols(gtf)$gene_id),
  feature_type = as.character(mcols(gtf)$type),
  product = as.character(mcols(gtf)$product),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

gtf_products <- gtf_products[
  !is.na(gtf_products$gene_id) &
    gtf_products$gene_id != "" &
    !is.na(gtf_products$product) &
    gtf_products$product != "",
]

product_annotation <- do.call(
  rbind,
  lapply(split(gtf_products, gtf_products$gene_id), function(x) {
    transcript_products <- unique(
      x$product[x$feature_type %in% c("transcript", "mRNA")]
    )

    products <- if (length(transcript_products) > 0) {
      transcript_products
    } else {
      unique(x$product)
    }

    data.frame(
      gene_id = x$gene_id[1],
      product_description = paste(products, collapse = " | "),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  })
)

rownames(product_annotation) <- NULL

gene_annotation$product_description <- product_annotation$product_description[
  match(gene_annotation$gene_id, product_annotation$gene_id)
]

message(
  sum(!is.na(gene_annotation$product_description)),
  " of ",
  nrow(gene_annotation),
  " count-table genes matched to a GTF product description."
)

rm(gtf, gtf_products, product_annotation)

count_data <- as.matrix(
  dat[, setdiff(names(dat), c("gene_id", "gene_name"))]
)

rownames(count_data) <- dat$gene_id
storage.mode(count_data) <- "numeric"

# DESeq2 requires nonnegative count-like input.
stopifnot(!anyNA(count_data))
stopifnot(all(count_data >= 0))

# The supplied merged file appears integer-valued. This also handles
# very small floating-point deviations if estimated counts are present.
if (!all(count_data == round(count_data))) {
  message("Non-integer estimated counts found; rounding for direct DESeq2 import.")
  count_data <- round(count_data)
}

# Remove annotation table from the working environment if desired.
rm(dat)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 2. Construct sample metadata
# ----------------------------

sample_id <- colnames(count_data)
sample_core <- sub("_S[0-9]+_L[0-9]+$", "", sample_id)

coldata <- data.frame(
  sample_id = sample_id,
  sample_core = sample_core,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

rownames(coldata) <- coldata$sample_id

coldata$day <- ifelse(
  grepl("^[CF]45-", coldata$sample_core), 45L,
  ifelse(grepl("^[CF]103-", coldata$sample_core), 103L, NA_integer_)
)

coldata$stage <- factor(
  ifelse(coldata$day == 45, "Young", "Old"),
  levels = c("Young", "Old")
)

coldata$treatment <- factor(
  ifelse(grepl("^F", coldata$sample_core), "Flutamide", "Control"),
  levels = c("Control", "Flutamide")
)

coldata$tissue <- factor(
  ifelse(grepl("B$", coldata$sample_core), "Body",
         ifelse(grepl("H$", coldata$sample_core), "Head", NA)),
  levels = c("Body", "Head")
)

# Tadpole identifier, retained for plotting and QC.
# Do not include it in the DGE model: tadpoles are nested within
# stage/treatment, so subject and group would be confounded.
coldata$tadpole <- sub("[BH]$", "", coldata$sample_core)

stopifnot(!anyNA(coldata[, c("day", "stage", "treatment", "tissue")]))
stopifnot(identical(rownames(coldata), colnames(count_data)))

# Combined experimental group. Using one factor makes the requested
# contrasts explicit and avoids unnecessary interaction complexity.
coldata$group <- factor(
  paste(coldata$stage, coldata$treatment, coldata$tissue, sep = "_"),
  levels = c(
    "Young_Control_Body",
    "Young_Control_Head",
    "Old_Control_Body",
    "Old_Control_Head",
    "Old_Flutamide_Body",
    "Old_Flutamide_Head"
  )
)

# Check replication and metadata assignment.
print(coldata[, c(
  "sample_core", "tadpole", "day",
  "stage", "treatment", "tissue", "group"
)])

print(table(coldata$group))

# Expected:
# Young_Control_Body     5
# Young_Control_Head     4
# Old_Control_Body       4
# Old_Control_Head       3
# Old_Flutamide_Body     5
# Old_Flutamide_Head     4
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 3. Build and fit DESeq2 model
# ----------------------------

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = count_data,
  colData = coldata,
  design = ~ group
)

# Attach gene names without using them as row identifiers.
# Gene names may not be unique; gene_id is the safer row identifier.
mcols(dds)$gene_name <- gene_annotation$gene_name[
  match(rownames(dds), gene_annotation$gene_id)
]

mcols(dds)$product_description <- gene_annotation$product_description[
  match(rownames(dds), gene_annotation$gene_id)
]

# Bulk RNA-seq prefilter recommended in the DESeq2 manual:
# count >= 10 in at least the smallest group size (3 samples).
keep <- rowSums(counts(dds) >= 10) >= 3
message(sum(keep), " of ", length(keep), " genes retained.")
dds <- dds[keep, ]

dds <- DESeq(dds)

resultsNames(dds)
plotDispEsts(dds)

# Create output directories.
dir.create("DESeq2_results", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("DESeq2_results/QC", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("DESeq2_results/DGE", showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("DESeq2_results/volcano", showWarnings = FALSE)

write.csv(
  as.data.frame(colData(dds)),
  "DESeq2_results/sample_metadata.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 4. VST for PCA and clustering
# ----------------------------

# blind=FALSE is appropriate for biological visualization when large,
# expected tissue/stage effects exist. It uses the fitted dispersion trend;
# it does not regress the group effects out of the transformed matrix.
vsd <- vst(dds, blind = FALSE)
vst_matrix <- assay(vsd)

# Select the 500 genes with greatest variance across samples.
gene_variance <- rowVars(vst_matrix)
names(gene_variance) <- rownames(vst_matrix)

n_top <- min(500, nrow(vst_matrix))
top500 <- names(sort(gene_variance, decreasing = TRUE))[seq_len(n_top)]

write.csv(
  data.frame(
    gene_id = top500,
    gene_name = mcols(dds)[top500, "gene_name"],
    VST_variance = gene_variance[top500]
  ),
  "DESeq2_results/QC/top500_variable_genes.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 5. PCA: top 500 variable genes
# ----------------------------

pca <- prcomp(t(vst_matrix[top500, , drop = FALSE]), scale. = FALSE)

percent_var <- 100 * pca$sdev^2 / sum(pca$sdev^2)

pca_data <- cbind(
  as.data.frame(pca$x[, 1:2, drop = FALSE]),
  as.data.frame(colData(vsd))
)

pca_plot <- ggplot(
  pca_data,
  aes(
    x = PC1,
    y = PC2,
    color = group,
    shape = tissue,
    label = sample_core
  )
) +
  geom_point(size = 3.5) +
  geom_text_repel(
    size = 3,
    max.overlaps = Inf,
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "PCA of all samples",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "VST-transformed counts; ",
      n_top,
      " most variable genes"
    ),
    x = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%%)", percent_var[1]),
    y = sprintf("PC2 (%.1f%%)", percent_var[2]),
    color = "Experimental group",
    shape = "Tissue"
  ) +
  coord_fixed() +
  theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")

ggsave(
  "DESeq2_results/QC/PCA_top500_variable_genes.pdf",
  pca_plot,
  width = 10,
  height = 7
)

ggsave(
  "DESeq2_results/QC/PCA_top500_variable_genes.png",
  pca_plot,
  width = 10,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)

write.csv(
  pca_data,
  "DESeq2_results/QC/PCA_sample_coordinates.csv",
  row.names = FALSE
)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 6. Sample-clustering dendrogram
# ----------------------------

# Euclidean distance across the same top 500 VST genes.
sample_dist <- dist(t(vst_matrix[top500, , drop = FALSE]))
sample_hc <- hclust(sample_dist, method = "complete")

sample_labels <- paste(
  coldata[sample_hc$labels, "sample_core"],
  coldata[sample_hc$labels, "group"],
  sep = " | "
)

pdf(
  "DESeq2_results/QC/sample_dendrogram_top500.pdf",
  width = 13,
  height = 7
)

plot(
  sample_hc,
  labels = sample_labels,
  main = "Hierarchical clustering of samples",
  sub = paste(n_top, "most variable VST-transformed genes"),
  xlab = "",
  ylab = "Euclidean distance",
  cex = 0.7,
  hang = -1
)

dev.off()

# Sample-to-sample distance heatmap.
sample_dist_matrix <- as.matrix(sample_dist)

annotation_col <- as.data.frame(
  colData(vsd)[, c("stage", "treatment", "tissue", "group")]
)

pheatmap(
  sample_dist_matrix,
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  annotation_row = annotation_col,
  clustering_distance_rows = sample_dist,
  clustering_distance_cols = sample_dist,
  filename = "DESeq2_results/QC/sample_distance_heatmap.pdf",
  width = 11,
  height = 10,
  main = "Sample distances: top 500 variable genes"
)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 7. Gene-expression heatmap
# ----------------------------

# For a readable gene-level heatmap, use the top 50 rather than all genes.
top50 <- top500[seq_len(min(50, length(top500)))]

heatmap_matrix <- vst_matrix[top50, , drop = FALSE]

# Row-wise z-scores show relative expression patterns per gene.
heatmap_z <- t(scale(t(heatmap_matrix)))
heatmap_z[!is.finite(heatmap_z)] <- 0

gene_labels <- mcols(dds)[top50, "gene_name"]
gene_labels <- ifelse(
  is.na(gene_labels) | gene_labels == "",
  top50,
  paste0(gene_labels, " [", top50, "]")
)

rownames(heatmap_z) <- make.unique(gene_labels)

pheatmap(
  heatmap_z,
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 6,
  fontsize_col = 7,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  clustering_method = "complete",
  color = colorRampPalette(c("navy", "white", "firebrick3"))(101),
  breaks = seq(-3, 3, length.out = 102),
  filename = "DESeq2_results/QC/top50_variable_gene_heatmap.pdf",
  width = 12,
  height = 12,
  main = "Top 50 variable genes (row z-scores)"
)
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 8. DGE and volcano function
# ----------------------------

run_comparison <- function(
  dds,
  numerator,
  denominator,
  comparison_name,
  padj_cutoff = 0.05,
  lfc_cutoff = 1
) {
  contrast_vector <- c("group", numerator, denominator)

  # Statistical results use the DESeq2 Wald test.
  res <- results(
    dds,
    contrast = contrast_vector,
    alpha = padj_cutoff
  )

  # Shrink LFCs for ranking and visualization.
  # ashr preserves large effects and accepts arbitrary contrasts.
  # If unavailable, DESeq2's built-in normal shrinker is used.
  if (requireNamespace("ashr", quietly = TRUE)) {
    res_shrunk <- lfcShrink(
      dds,
      contrast = contrast_vector,
      res = res,
      type = "ashr"
    )
    shrink_method <- "ashr"
  } else {
    warning(
      "Package 'ashr' is unavailable; using DESeq2 type='normal'. ",
      "Install ashr for the preferred wide-tailed shrinkage estimator."
    )
    res_shrunk <- lfcShrink(
      dds,
      contrast = contrast_vector,
      res = res,
      type = "normal"
    )
    shrink_method <- "normal"
  }

  out <- as.data.frame(res)
  out$gene_id <- rownames(out)
  out$gene_name <- mcols(dds)[out$gene_id, "gene_name"]
  out$product_description <- mcols(dds)[
    out$gene_id,
    "product_description"
  ]
  out$shrunken_log2FoldChange <- res_shrunk$log2FoldChange
  out$shrunken_lfcSE <- res_shrunk$lfcSE

  # Put identifiers first.
  out <- out[, c(
    "gene_id",
    "gene_name",
    "product_description",
    "baseMean",
    "log2FoldChange",
    "lfcSE",
    "shrunken_log2FoldChange",
    "shrunken_lfcSE",
    "stat",
    "pvalue",
    "padj"
  )]

  out <- out[order(out$padj, out$pvalue, na.last = TRUE), ]

  out$significance <- "Not significant"
  out$significance[
    !is.na(out$padj) &
      out$padj < padj_cutoff &
      out$shrunken_log2FoldChange >= lfc_cutoff
  ] <- "Up"

  out$significance[
    !is.na(out$padj) &
      out$padj < padj_cutoff &
      out$shrunken_log2FoldChange <= -lfc_cutoff
  ] <- "Down"

  write.csv(
    out,
    file.path(
      "DESeq2_results/DGE",
      paste0(comparison_name, "_DESeq2_results.csv")
    ),
    row.names = FALSE
  )

  # For plotting only, NA adjusted p-values are treated as nonsignificant.
  # DESeq2 may produce NA padj for low-information genes or outliers.
  plot_data <- out
  plot_data$plot_padj <- ifelse(
    is.na(plot_data$padj),
    1,
    pmax(plot_data$padj, .Machine$double.xmin)
  )

  # Label the 10 most significant genes meeting both thresholds.
  label_candidates <- subset(
    plot_data,
    significance != "Not significant" &
      !is.na(gene_name) &
      gene_name != ""
  )

  label_candidates <- head(
    label_candidates[order(label_candidates$padj), ],
    10
  )

  volcano <- ggplot(
    plot_data,
    aes(
      x = shrunken_log2FoldChange,
      y = -log10(plot_padj),
      color = significance
    )
  ) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.65, size = 1.5) +
    geom_vline(
      xintercept = c(-lfc_cutoff, lfc_cutoff),
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "grey40"
    ) +
    geom_hline(
      yintercept = -log10(padj_cutoff),
      linetype = "dashed",
      color = "grey40"
    ) +
    geom_text_repel(
      data = label_candidates,
      aes(label = gene_name),
      size = 3,
      box.padding = 0.4,
      max.overlaps = Inf,
      show.legend = FALSE
    ) +
    scale_color_manual(
      values = c(
        "Down" = "#2C7BB6",
        "Not significant" = "grey75",
        "Up" = "#D7191C"
      ),
      breaks = c("Up", "Down", "Not significant")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = comparison_name,
      subtitle = paste0(
        numerator,
        " vs ",
        denominator,
        "; LFC shrinkage: ",
        shrink_method
      ),
      x = "Shrunken log2 fold change",
      y = expression(-log[10]("adjusted p-value")),
      color = NULL,
      caption = paste0(
        "Significant: adjusted p < ",
        padj_cutoff,
        " and |shrunken log2FC| \u2265 ",
        lfc_cutoff
      )
    ) +
    theme_classic(base_size = 12) +
    theme(legend.position = "top")

  ggsave(
    file.path(
      "DESeq2_results/volcano",
      paste0(comparison_name, "_volcano.pdf")
    ),
    volcano,
    width = 8,
    height = 7
  )

  ggsave(
    file.path(
      "DESeq2_results/volcano",
      paste0(comparison_name, "_volcano.png")
    ),
    volcano,
    width = 8,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )

  message(
    comparison_name,
    ": ",
    sum(out$significance == "Up", na.rm = TRUE),
    " up and ",
    sum(out$significance == "Down", na.rm = TRUE),
    " down"
  )

  return(out)
}
```


```{r}
# ----------------------------
# 9. Requested comparisons
# ----------------------------

# Positive LFC = higher expression in OLD control body.
leg_emergence <- run_comparison(
  dds = dds,
  numerator = "Old_Control_Body",
  denominator = "Young_Control_Body",
  comparison_name = "leg_emergence_OldControlBody_vs_YoungControlBody"
)

# Positive LFC = higher expression in OLD control head.
nervous_system_development <- run_comparison(
  dds = dds,
  numerator = "Old_Control_Head",
  denominator = "Young_Control_Head",
  comparison_name = "nervous_system_OldControlHead_vs_YoungControlHead"
)

# Positive LFC = higher expression after FLUTAMIDE treatment.
ar_blockade_body <- run_comparison(
  dds = dds,
  numerator = "Old_Flutamide_Body",
  denominator = "Old_Control_Body",
  comparison_name = "AR_blockade_OldBodyFlutamide_vs_OldBodyControl"
)

# Positive LFC = higher expression after FLUTAMIDE treatment in head tissue.
ar_blockade_head <- run_comparison(
  dds = dds,
  numerator = "Old_Flutamide_Head",
  denominator = "Old_Control_Head",
  comparison_name = "AR_blockade_OldHeadFlutamide_vs_OldHeadControl"
)

# Save the fitted object so analysis need not be rerun.
saveRDS(dds, "DESeq2_results/dds_fitted.rds")
saveRDS(vsd, "DESeq2_results/vsd.rds")

sessionInfo()
```
